forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,47 @@
+---
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: a080
+project: cli-schema-gen
+title: Migrate review tool to wire/full schema split + document the convention so new operation tools comply
+---
+## Context
+
+The `cli-schema-gen` project split every operation-based MCP tool's schema into two surfaces (`crates/swissarmyhammer-operations/src/schema.rs`):
+
+- **WIRE** (`generate_mcp_schema_wire`) — model-facing, sent over MCP `tools/list`. Carries only the `op` enum + `x-op-signatures`. Deliberately DROPS the heavy keys (`x-operation-schemas`, `x-operation-groups`, `x-forgiving-input`, `examples`) — see `WIRE_DROPPED_KEYS`.
+- **FULL** (`generate_mcp_schema_full`, aliased as `generate_mcp_schema`) — in-process only, consumed by the shared CLI generator (`cli_gen::build_commands_from_schema`, which reads `x-operation-schemas`). Carries the heavy keys.
+
+The `McpTool` trait wires this via two methods (`tool_registry.rs`): `schema()` returns the WIRE surface (goes over the wire), `schema_full()` returns the FULL surface (default falls back to `schema()`; CLI build calls `schema_full()` at `tool_registry.rs` `build_tool_subcommand`).
+
+**10 tools already comply** (override `schema_full()`): questions, agent, ralph, web, kanban, code_context, git, shell, files, skill.
+
+**`review` is the lone holdout.** `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/review/mod.rs` (`impl McpTool for ReviewTool`, ~line 414) returns `generate_mcp_schema(&REVIEW_OPERATIONS, config)` from `schema()` and never overrides `schema_full()`. Because `generate_mcp_schema` == `generate_mcp_schema_full`, review ships the heavy `x-operation-schemas`/`x-operation-groups`/`examples` over the wire on every prompt — exactly what the project set out to stop. (This is why the `mcp__sah__review` tool definition shows `x-operation-schemas` while every other operation tool shows the slim `x-op-signatures`.)
+
+Two reinforcing gaps let this slip and will let the next tool slip too:
+1. `standards/mcp.md` (the "Schema Generation" section + the `McpTool` example + the "Checklist for New Tools") still instructs implementers to use `generate_mcp_schema()` and documents the full-schema output (`x-operation-schemas`, `x-operation-groups`) as the wire surface — actively steering new tools the wrong way.
+2. There is no workspace-wide guard test asserting the split for *all* operation tools; per-tool tests exist but a brand-new tool that forgets `schema_full()` passes CI.
+
+Out of scope: `crates/swissarmyhammer-js/src/schema.rs::generate_js_mcp_schema` uses the full generator but has no `McpTool` impl (not a wire surface) — leave it, but its doc comment may be touched by subtask 4 if convenient.
+
+## What
+
+- [ ] **Migrate `review`** in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/review/mod.rs`: change the import from `generate_mcp_schema` to `generate_mcp_schema_full, generate_mcp_schema_wire`; make `schema()` return `generate_mcp_schema_wire(&REVIEW_OPERATIONS, config)`; add a `schema_full()` override returning `generate_mcp_schema_full(&REVIEW_OPERATIONS, config)` (factor the `SchemaConfig` construction into a small private helper so wire and full share one config, matching the `*_schema_config()` pattern used by the migrated tools, e.g. `kanban/schema.rs`).
+- [ ] **Document the convention** in `standards/mcp.md`: rewrite the "Schema Generation" section and the `McpTool` trait example so they state the split explicitly — `schema()` returns the slim WIRE schema (`generate_mcp_schema_wire`, carries `x-op-signatures`, drops the heavy keys); `schema_full()` returns the FULL schema (`generate_mcp_schema_full`, carries `x-operation-schemas`/`x-operation-groups` for in-process CLI generation). Update the "Checklist for New Tools" to require both methods. Make clear the heavy keys must NEVER ship over the wire.
+- [ ] **Add a steering comment** on `generate_mcp_schema` (the full alias) in `crates/swissarmyhammer-operations/src/schema.rs` and/or the `schema()`/`schema_full()` doc comments in `tool_registry.rs`, pointing new operation-tool authors at the wire/full split so the next implementer cannot use the full generator for `schema()` by accident.
+- [ ] **Add a workspace-wide guard test** (see Tests) that mechanically enforces the split for every registered operation-based MCP tool.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] `ReviewTool::schema()` returns a schema whose object has NO `x-operation-schemas`, NO `x-operation-groups`, and DOES have `x-op-signatures`.
+- [ ] `ReviewTool::schema_full()` returns a schema that DOES have `x-operation-schemas` and `x-operation-groups`.
+- [ ] `sah tool review ...` CLI still builds and exposes the same noun/verb tree with per-op arg scoping (review file/working/sha, list/get/check validators) — proven by the CLI command-tree test, not manual run.
+- [ ] `standards/mcp.md` documents `schema()` = wire and `schema_full()` = full, and the checklist requires both; no remaining text tells implementers to put the full schema on the wire.
+- [ ] A guard test fails if any operation-based tool's `schema()` leaks a `WIRE_DROPPED_KEYS` key.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Add per-tool wire/full assertions for review in `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/review/mod.rs` tests, mirroring the pattern in `web/mod.rs` / `shell/mod.rs` (`wire["x-op-signatures"].is_object()`, `schema().get("x-operation-schemas").is_none()`, `schema_full()["x-operation-schemas"].is_array()`).
+- [ ] Add a workspace-wide guard in `apps/swissarmyhammer-cli/tests/integration/mcp_tools_registration.rs`: build the real `ToolRegistry`, iterate every tool with a non-empty `operations()`, and assert for each that `schema()` (wire) omits all `swissarmyhammer_operations::WIRE_DROPPED_KEYS` and contains `x-op-signatures`, while `schema_full()` contains `x-operation-schemas` + `x-operation-groups`. Import `WIRE_DROPPED_KEYS` rather than re-listing the keys.
+- [ ] Extend/confirm the review CLI command-tree coverage test (the `build_commands_from_schema` / `collect_verb_noun_pairs` pattern in `cli_gen::test_support`) asserts review's verbs still resolve after the migration.
+- [ ] Run: `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-tools review:: && cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-cli --test integration mcp_tools_registration` — expected: green, and the new guard test fails first if `review`'s `schema_full()` override is reverted (verify RED→GREEN).
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write the failing guard test (it should fail on `review` today) first, then migrate review to make it pass, then update the docs.
\ No newline at end of file
